sentences_input.text: I don't know in this period how much [? cautionately ?] I was realizing that I really loved to be involved with-- [BACKROUND NOISE] with music, musical art at the time.

I only felt that this for me was presenting some form of communication, some form of being able to touch the people, be able to-- to watch the faces that were communicating, the eyes that were communicating with me, and the time in this way that-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] I was almost feeling happy at the time that I'm able to perform my little thing without many mistakes.

Perhaps these experiences later on left in me this very special sensation of performing in front of audiences, [BACKROUND NOISE] and be able to feel the communication, this invisible spirit of emotional communication with your audience-- audiences.

[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] This also eventually gave me the first [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] powerful lessons of responsibility of [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] performance that is without mistakes when you are in front of a big group of people.

But more than that, it is desire to be able to communicate with them something that is familiar to them that they like it, and it's telling them something that they not only enjoy, but they react in this so emotional way.
Of course, it was quite well before that my really serious studies will resume and begin, studies with music, studies-- studies with [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] one instrument like violin.
This come considerably later in my life, when [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] we were able already to settle ourselves and leave the-- this small village.
